core.appIs: tic-tac-toe — a two-player m,n,k (3,3,3) game served as a web arena
core.goal: place three of your own marks in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal); otherwise draw
core.howToJoin: take a seat by submitting the first move for a side; the first mover on each side is seated (X moves first, then O); later arrivals are observers

myAffordances: make a move on X's turn to an empty square

expectedPositionTokenSource.note: A=1 gates the active form (seat+turn), so an unseated agent is pushed toward /profile; board labels remain a valid source. C makes the vocabulary explicit on the board via aria-labels ('{position}, {state}').

expectedArtifactDiscovery: ARIA gridcell roles + nav landmark + live region; squares accessibly labeled '{position}, {state}' — the position vocabulary (TopLeft..BottomRight) exposed to assistive tech
the /profile contract (advertised via Link: rel=profile) enumerating make-move (player X/O + the 9 named positions); Allow/OPTIONS on the arena
schema.org Game JSON-LD typing block identifying the app as a Game
notes: Factors on: ACSdSo. A=1: move forms only for the caller's currently-legal squares (seat+turn-gated). C=1: accessible structure; position vocabulary announced via aria-labels. Sd=1: /profile contract + Link/Allow headers expose the move format (player + 9 positions). So=1: schema.org Game ontology typing.
